input.question:
We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Please list all rules relevant to `Material properties/material/materials`. Answer with only the rule numbers (i.e.: AA.1.1.1) separated by commas and no other words.



The rules relevant to `Material properties/material/materials` are:

retrieved_context:
RULE: f.3.5.2
            TITLE: if any alternative materials are used, the ses must contain:

            CONTENT:
            rule F.3.5.2: If any Alternative Materials are used, the SES must contain:
F.3.5.2 If any Alternative Materials are used, the SES must contain: 
a. Documentation of material type, (purchase receipt, shipping document or letter of 
donation) and the material properties.  
b. Calculations that show equivalent to or better than the minimum requirements for steel 
tubing in the application as listed in F.3.4.1 for yield and ultimate strengths matching the 
Non Welded Steel properties from F.3.4.2.a above in bending, buckling and tension, for 
buckling modulus and for energy dissipation 
c. Details of the manufacturing technique and process

RULE: f.10.1.4
            TITLE: if the accumulator container is not constructed from steel or aluminum, the material

            CONTENT:
            rule F.10.1.4: If the Accumulator Container is not constructed from steel or aluminum, the material
F.10.1.4 If the Accumulator Container is not constructed from steel or aluminum, the material 
properties should be established at a temperature of 60°C

RULE: f.4.1.1
            TITLE: documentation of material type, (purchase receipt, shipping document or letter of donation)

            CONTENT:
            rule F.4.1.1: Documentation of material type, (purchase receipt, shipping document or letter of donation)
F.4.1.1 Documentation of material type, (purchase receipt, shipping document or letter of donation) 
and the material properties.

RULE: f.3.4.2
            TITLE: properties for any steel material for calculations submitted in an ses must be:

            CONTENT:
            rule F.3.4.2: Properties for ANY steel material for calculations submitted in an SES must be:
F.3.4.2 Properties for ANY steel material for calculations submitted in an SES must be: 
a. Non Welded Properties for continuous material calculations: 
Young’s Modulus (E) = 200 GPa (29,000 ksi)  
Yield Strength (Sy) = 305 MPa (44.2 ksi)  
Ultimate Strength (Su) = 365 MPa (52.9 ksi) 
b. Welded Properties for discontinuous material such as joint calculations:  
Yield Strength (Sy) = 180 MPa (26 ksi)  
Ultimate Strength (Su) = 300 MPa (43.5 ksi)

RULE: f.1.18
            TITLE: nonflammable material

            CONTENT:
            rule F.1.18: Nonflammable Material
F.1.18 Nonflammable Material  
Metal or a Non Metallic material which meets UL94-V0, FAR25 or approved equivalent

RULE: f.5.1.1
            TITLE: the primary structure must be constructed from one or a combination of the following:

            CONTENT:
            rule F.5.1.1: The Primary Structure must be constructed from one or a combination of the following:
F.5.1.1 The Primary Structure must be constructed from one or a combination of the following: 
• Steel Tubing and Material  F.3.2   F.3.4 
• Alternative Tubing Materials F.3.2 F.3.5 
• Composite Material  F.4

RULE: t.5.2.4
            TITLE: frame members or existing components that exceed the scatter shield material requirements

            CONTENT:
            rule T.5.2.4: Frame Members or existing components that exceed the scatter shield material requirements
T.5.2.4 Frame Members or existing components that exceed the scatter shield material requirements 
may be used as part of the shield.

RULE: s.3.14.1
            TITLE: teams may be selected for additional review to verify all processes and materials on their

            CONTENT:
            rule S.3.14.1: Teams may be selected for additional review to verify all processes and materials on their
S.3.14.1 Teams may be selected for additional review to verify all processes and materials on their 
vehicle are in the Cost Report

RULE: f.2.3.3
            TITLE: the properties of tubes and laminates may be combined to prove equivalence.

            CONTENT:
            rule F.2.3.3: The properties of tubes and laminates may be combined to prove Equivalence.
F.2.3.3 The properties of tubes and laminates may be combined to prove Equivalence.  
For example, in a Side Impact Structure consisting of one tube per F.3.2.1.e and a laminate 
panel, the panel only needs to be Equivalent to two Side Impact Tubes.

RULE: t.6.1.6
            TITLE: cylinder material – gas cylinders/tanks in a position 150 mm or less from an exhaust system

            CONTENT:
            rule T.6.1.6: Cylinder Material – gas cylinders/tanks in a position 150 mm or less from an exhaust system
T.6.1.6 Cylinder Material – gas cylinders/tanks in a position 150 mm or less from an exhaust system 
must meet one of the two: 
• Made from metal
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 69 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
• Meet the thermal protection requirements of T.1.6.3

RULE: ev.6.2.1
            TITLE: insulation material must:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.6.2.1: Insulation material must:
EV.6.2.1 Insulation material must:  
a. Be appropriate for the expected surrounding temperatures 
b. Have a minimum temperature rating of 90°C

RULE: ev.6.1.1
            TITLE: nonconductive material or covers must prevent inadvertent human contact with any tractive

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.6.1.1: Nonconductive material or covers must prevent inadvertent human contact with any Tractive
EV.6.1.1 Nonconductive material or covers must prevent inadvertent human contact with any Tractive 
System voltage.  
Covers must be secure and sufficiently rigid.   
Removable Bodywork is not suitable to enclose Tractive System connections.

RULE: f.6.3.1
            TITLE: front hoop braces must be constructed of material meeting f.3.2.1.d

            CONTENT:
            rule F.6.3.1: Front Hoop Braces must be constructed of material meeting F.3.2.1.d
F.6.3.1 Front Hoop Braces must be constructed of material meeting F.3.2.1.d

RULE: t.2.4.2
            TITLE: any guide or support for the belts must be material meeting f.3.2.1.j

            CONTENT:
            rule T.2.4.2: Any guide or support for the belts must be material meeting F.3.2.1.j
T.2.4.2 Any guide or support for the belts must be material meeting F.3.2.1.j

RULE: t.1.7.3
            TITLE: the panel(s) must be made of a solid, non brittle material

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.7.3: The panel(s) must be made of a solid, non brittle material
T.1.7.3 The panel(s) must be made of a solid, non brittle material

RULE: f.4.1.2
            TITLE: details of the manufacturing technique and/or composite layup technique as well as the

            CONTENT:
            rule F.4.1.2: Details of the manufacturing technique and/or composite layup technique as well as the
F.4.1.2 Details of the manufacturing technique and/or composite layup technique as well as the 
structural material used (examples - cloth type, weight, and resin type, number of layers, core 
material, and skin material if metal).

RULE: f.7.1.5
            TITLE: composite monocoques must:

            CONTENT:
            rule F.7.1.5: Composite monocoques must:
F.7.1.5 Composite monocoques must: 
a. Meet the materials requirements in F.4 Composite and Other Materials 
b. Use data from the laminate testing results as the basis for any strength or stiffness 
calculations

RULE: f.4.1
            TITLE: requirements

            CONTENT:
            rule F.4.1: Requirements
F.4.1 Requirements 
If any composite or other material is used, the SES must contain:

RULE: f.4.2
            TITLE: quasi-isotropic layup

            CONTENT:
            rule F.4.2: Quasi-Isotropic Layup
F.4.2 Quasi-Isotropic Layup 
A layup with equal fiber strength and stiffness along any orientation in the plane of the layup.  
a. When a layup has equal fiber properties and mass in the 0/90/+45/-45 directions, the 
layup may be considered Quasi-Isotropic

RULE: t.5.5.5
            TITLE: absorbent material and open collection devices (regardless of material) are prohibited in

            CONTENT:
            rule T.5.5.5: Absorbent material and open collection devices (regardless of material) are prohibited in
T.5.5.5 Absorbent material and open collection devices (regardless of material) are prohibited in 
compartments containing engine, drivetrain, exhaust and fuel systems below the highest 
point on the exhaust system.
